input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
## AppThemeBinding in .NET MAUI

**XAML approach** — use `AppThemeBinding` to specify Light/Dark values inline:

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="16">
        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="24"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#F0F0F0}" />

        <Label Text="This page adapts to your system theme."
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#333333, Dark=#CCCCCC}" />

        <Button Text="Click Me"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

The framework automatically re-evaluates these bindings when the OS theme changes — no event wiring needed.

---

**C# alternative** — use `SetAppTheme` or manually check `Application.Current.RequestedTheme`:

```csharp
// Option 1: SetAppThemeColor / SetAppTheme (preferred)
myLabel.SetAppThemeColor(Label.TextColorProperty, Colors.Black, Colors.White);
myButton.SetAppTheme(Button.BackgroundColorProperty,
    Color.FromArgb("#6200EE"), Color.FromArgb("#BB86FC"));

// Option 2: React to theme changes manually
Application.Current.RequestedThemeChanged += (s, e) =>
{
    bool isDark = e.RequestedTheme == AppTheme.Dark;
    BackgroundColor = isDark ? Color.FromArgb("#1E1E1E") : Colors.White;
};
```

---

**Key points:**
- `AppThemeBinding` responds to `Application.Current.UserAppTheme` (if set) or the OS setting.
- For reusable colors, define them in a `ResourceDictionary` with `AppThemeBinding` and reference via `{StaticResource}`.
- No platform-specific code is required — this works on all MAUI targets.
data.reasoningText: The user is asking for help with .NET MAUI theming using `AppThemeBinding`. This is a knowledge/explanation question rather than a code task on an existing repository. I'll provide a concise but complete example showing both XAML and C# approaches.
